headline: Exclusive-Italy seeks payment from Meta, X and LinkedIn in landmark tax case
summary: MILAN (Reuters) -Italy has handed tax demands to Meta, X and LinkedIn in the last formal step in an unprecedented VAT claim against the  U.S. tech giants that could have repercussions across the European Union, four sources with direct knowledge of the matter said on Wednesday.  While it has been reported that Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta and Elon Musks social network X were under investigation for alleged tax fraud, it had not been disclosed that Microsofts LinkedIn unit was also caught up in Italys pilot VAT case for the tech sector in Europe.

headline: Why Meta Platforms Stock Was Pulling Back Today
summary: Shares of Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) were pulling back today on a combination of a broad-based sell-off related to the upcoming Liberation Day round of tariffs, and as one analyst lowered its price target on the stock, weighing on the shifting macroeconomic climate.  As one of the most valuable companies in the world and a global leader in digital advertising, Meta Platforms has significant exposure to the health of the global economy.  Advertising is one of the first expenses businesses tend to cut back on when they sense that a recession is coming, and Meta has felt that impact, including in 2022, when its revenue briefly declined.
